description:
## Background

Issue Detail renders Parent, Blocked by, Blocking, and Child Issue IDs as read-only text. These are Issue References within the Current Workspace and should support the same navigation semantics as Issue List rows.

Triage: `ready-for-agent`.

## What to build

Make every rendered Issue Reference navigate to its Issue in the Current Workspace. A reference click creates one Issue Navigation Entry, reveals a hidden target by using All Issues and clearing search when necessary, and renders the shared Issue-not-found state when the reference is stale.

## Technical Approach

- Create one reusable Issue Reference link presentation using Wouter Link semantics.
- Apply it to Parent, Blocked by, Blocking, and Child Issue entries without changing their metadata meaning or visual hierarchy.
- Generate the destination from the referenced Issue ID and Current Workspace navigation context.
- If the target exists but is excluded by the active Issue List View or Issue Search, navigate to All Issues with search cleared so its row is visible.
- Preserve the prior destination in history so Back restores the originating Issue, view, and search.
- If the target ID is absent from All Issues, keep the target route and render the existing Issue-not-found detail state.
- Ensure a reference to the already-current Issue is a no-op rather than a duplicate entry.
- Keep external Markdown links governed by the existing safe external-link policy; plain Issue IDs in authored Markdown do not become links in this slice.

## Considerations

- Issue References are scoped to the Current Workspace; they never trigger a Workspace confirmation.
- Child ordering and derivation from All Issues must remain unchanged.
- Reference links should retain real anchor keyboard, modifier, and context-menu behavior.
- Future Issue Reference presentations should be able to reuse the same component/contract.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Parent, Blocked by, Blocking, and Child Issue IDs render as accessible links.
- [ ] Activating a reference opens the target Issue and creates exactly one navigation entry.
- [ ] Hidden targets are revealed through All Issues with search cleared as needed.
- [ ] Back restores the exact originating Issue, view, and search.
- [ ] Missing references render the shared Issue-not-found state while keeping their route.
- [ ] Activating a reference to the current Issue does not duplicate history.
- [ ] Child ordering, dependency copy, and authored Markdown link safety remain unchanged.

## Test Plan

- Update Issue Detail tests from read-only reference expectations to accessible link and navigation expectations.
- Cover each reference category, hidden targets, missing targets, current-target no-op, and Back restoration.
- Retain child ordering and external Markdown safety regression tests.
- Run renderer tests, TypeScript build, and Ultracite.
title: Navigate Issue References
